name: Samsung 990 PRO Heatsink SSD 4TB NVMe M.2
shortDescription: The Samsung 990 PRO 4TB is the highest-capacity PCIe 4.0 NVMe SSD in Samsung's lineup — purpose-built for AI builders who store multiple large model families without constant weight management. At 7,450 MB/s sequential read, model loading times are negligible even for 70B quantized weights.
useCases: Storing multiple LLM model families without disk management — 70B, 32B, 13B, 7B simultaneously
Fast model loading — 70B Q4 weights (~40GB) load in under 6 seconds
Stable Diffusion model library — SDXL, FLUX.1, SD 3.5, and LoRA collections
AI workstation primary drive with OS, tools, and model cache on one disk
PS5 expansion for console AI gaming workflows
pros: 4TB capacity — enough for 10+ large model families without rotating weights
7,450 MB/s sequential read — fastest PCIe 4.0 SSD, loads 13B model in ~1.5 seconds
Included heatsink — prevents thermal throttling during sustained model loading
Samsung MLC NAND — proven longevity and endurance for write-heavy AI caching
Works in any PCIe 4.0 or 5.0 M.2 slot — universal compatibility
cons: PCIe 4.0 — the Samsung 9100 PRO is twice as fast on PCIe 5.0 systems
Premium price per TB vs DRAM-less budget NVMe options
Heatsink adds height — may not fit under some GPU coolers in tight builds
verdict: For AI workstation builders, storage capacity is the underrated bottleneck. The Samsung 990 PRO 4TB eliminates model weight management entirely — store every model you actively use, load any of them in seconds, and never delete to free space. The included heatsink and Samsung's reliability track record make this the default storage recommendation for any serious local AI build.
notFor: PCIe 5.0 system owners — the Samsung 9100 PRO doubles read speed on Gen 5 slots
Budget builders — a 2TB PCIe 4.0 option costs significantly less with half the capacity
Mac Mini users — M.2 is not user-accessible on Apple Silicon Mac Minis
entityHeadline: Samsung 990 PRO 4TB: The Right Storage for a Local AI Model Library

faq.question: How many AI models fit on a 4TB NVMe SSD?
faq.answer: A 4TB drive comfortably stores: all Llama 3 variants (8B, 70B Q4, 70B Q8) at ~60GB total, multiple Mistral and Qwen2.5 families, a full Stable Diffusion library including SDXL, FLUX.1 dev, FLUX.1 schnell, and SD 3.5 with LoRA collections (~100GB), plus your OS and tools. Most builders never fill 4TB with model weights alone.

faq.question: Is PCIe 4.0 fast enough for AI model loading?
faq.answer: Yes. At 7,450 MB/s, the 990 PRO loads a 13B Q4 model (~9GB) in approximately 1.2 seconds and a 70B Q4 model (~40GB) in about 5–6 seconds. PCIe 5.0 is roughly twice as fast in peak sequential reads, but the real-world difference in model load times is under 3 seconds for most models — not meaningful for typical usage.
